# Security Requirements Checklist: User Authentication

**Purpose**: Validate authentication, session, token, tenant-scope, and security requirement quality before implementation
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are login-only scope boundaries complete enough to prevent account registration, password reset, MFA, SSO, or user-management requirements from leaking into this feature? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for every authentication state that the feature names: anonymous, signed in, access-expired, refresh-eligible, refresh-rejected, signed out, revoked, and suspicious reuse? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005-FR-014]
- [ ] CHK003 Are credential protection requirements documented for password storage, password non-disclosure, token non-disclosure, and audit-log redaction? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, §FR-020, §FR-025]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements complete for all three user types (`MASTER`, `ORGANIZATION`, `STANDARD`) across login, session summary, tenant scope, and seeded verification data? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022, §FR-026, §FR-028]
- [ ] CHK005 Are server-recognized session requirements complete for creation, last-used tracking, expiration, revocation status, and browser/device context? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §UserSession]
- [ ] CHK006 Are renewable session requirements complete for creation, rotation, rejection, revocation, and reuse detection? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, §FR-009-FR-011, Data Model §RefreshToken]
- [ ] CHK007 Are audit event requirements complete for success, failure, refresh, sign-out, invalidation, authorization rejection, and reuse detection without recording secrets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020, Data Model §AuthenticationAuditEvent]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK008 Is the password policy stated with unambiguous acceptance criteria, including whether uppercase letters and symbols are allowed but not required? [Clarity, Spec §FR-027]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "generic authentication message" defined clearly enough to prevent user enumeration while still allowing user-friendly validation errors for malformed input? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002-FR-003]
- [ ] CHK010 Are "short-lived access state" and "renewable session state" defined with enough distinction to avoid ambiguity between access credential, refresh credential, and server session? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006-FR-007, Data Model §AccessToken]
- [ ] CHK011 Are token lifetime, refresh lifetime, and rotation interval requirements quantified or explicitly deferred to planning with acceptance criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK012 Is the meaning of "inactive organization" clear for both login rejection and refresh/current-session rejection? [Clarity, Spec §US1, §US2, §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK013 Is the expected organization scope for `ORGANIZATION` and `STANDARD` seed users clear enough when no organization exists yet? [Ambiguity, Data Model §User Seed users]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK014 Are the spec, plan, data model, and tasks consistent that the authentication user table owns login identity only and does not include role-specific profile data? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Structure Decision, Data Model §User]
- [ ] CHK015 Are requirements consistent that existing production endpoints must remain unmarked by the new authorization marker until a later access-policy feature? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Constraints, Tasks §US4]
- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements consistent between cookie-based browser session storage and the rule that secret token material must not be exposed to client-side scripts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, §FR-025, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK017 Are shared contract requirements consistent across sign-in, current session, refresh, logout, authenticated user summary, and structured auth errors? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, §FR-023, Contracts §shared-types]
- [ ] CHK018 Are tenant-scope requirements consistent between user account scope, token/session claims, session summary, and future authorization context? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Tenant Isolation, Data Model §AccessToken]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK019 Are success criteria specific enough to measure valid login completion, invalid credential rejection, session continuity, sign-out completion, concurrent sessions, secret non-disclosure, and authorization marker behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-007]
- [ ] CHK020 Can the "under 30 seconds" sign-in outcome be objectively measured without mixing user typing time with system responsiveness? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK021 Is the "under 5 seconds" sign-out outcome tied to a defined start and end condition for the requirement? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK022 Is the five concurrent sessions requirement defined with enough scope to clarify whether it is a minimum supported count, a limit, or a validation target? [Clarity, Spec §SC-005, §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK023 Is the 100% rejection requirement for invalid sessions measurable across missing, expired, revoked, malformed, reused, inactive-user, and inactive-organization cases? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, §SC-007]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK024 Are primary, alternate, exception, and recovery scenarios documented for login attempts, including valid credentials, wrong password, unknown email, inactive user, inactive organization, and network interruption? [Coverage, Spec §US1, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are recovery requirements defined for refresh failure so the browser session, server session, and user-facing signed-out state remain aligned? [Coverage, Spec §US2, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements defined for signing in when an older session already exists in the same browser? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements defined for multiple active sessions and single-session invalidation without unintended cross-session revocation? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012-FR-014, §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements defined for suspicious refresh reuse, including whether only the affected session or broader user sessions are invalidated? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011, Data Model §RefreshToken]
- [ ] CHK029 Are requirements defined for how platform-scoped `MASTER` sessions differ from tenant-scoped `ORGANIZATION` and `STANDARD` sessions in future protected access? [Coverage, Spec §FR-022, §US4]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK030 Are security requirements specified for secret handling across responses, logs, browser storage, analytics, seed data, and audit metadata? [Security, Spec §FR-020, §FR-025]
- [ ] CHK031 Are performance requirements for login, current-session lookup, and refresh sufficiently quantified for the user-facing flows? [Clarity, Plan §Performance Goals, Spec §SC-001, §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK032 Are privacy/data-retention requirements for authentication audit events, IP address, user agent, and attempted email documented or intentionally deferred? [Gap, Data Model §AuthenticationAuditEvent]
- [ ] CHK033 Are brute-force, rate-limiting, lockout, or abuse-prevention requirements intentionally excluded or missing for the login endpoint? [Gap, Spec §FR-003, §FR-020]
- [ ] CHK034 Are accessibility requirements for the sign-in and sign-out UI documented or intentionally deferred for this auth feature? [Gap, Spec §US1, §US3]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK035 Are assumptions about seeded users, demo organization scope, and future provisioning documented clearly enough for local validation without creating public registration requirements? [Assumption, Spec §FR-028, §Assumptions, Data Model §User Seed users]
- [ ] CHK036 Are dependency requirements for cryptographic secrets, token lifetime configuration, and cookie/session configuration documented with ownership boundaries between API and web? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [ ] CHK037 Are shared-contract ownership requirements documented clearly enough to prevent web or API packages from importing private internals? [Dependency, Constitution §I-II, Plan §Shared Contracts]
- [ ] CHK038 Are migration and seed-data requirements documented without conflating stable schema migrations with local demo user provisioning? [Consistency, Research §Seed one local test user, Tasks §T010-T012]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK039 Is there any conflict between technology-neutral spec language and plan-level decisions requiring specific tools for tokens, password hashing, and browser cookie sessions? [Conflict, Spec §FR-004, §FR-006-FR-008, Plan §Primary Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK040 Is the authorization marker described clearly enough for the current route-module architecture while still matching the user's "decorator" language? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-016-FR-019, Research §Authorization marker]
- [ ] CHK041 Are requirements explicit about which current temporary Master-header behavior remains in place until protected endpoint policy is defined? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-019, Tasks §T038, §T063]
- [ ] CHK042 Are requirement IDs, task IDs, and contract sections traceable enough for reviewers to map every critical auth requirement to at least one implementation task? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001-FR-028, Tasks §T001-T078]
